sourceMovie.title: Freaky Friday
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Freaky Friday for the same reason it broke through in 2003: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a fantasy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Mark Waters keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The family and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Addams Family Values
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Freaky Friday clicked for you, Addams Family Values (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Slumberland
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Freaky Friday clicked for you, Slumberland (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Freaky Friday?
faq.answer: Try Addams Family Values, Slumberland, Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical—each shares DNA with Freaky Friday while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Freaky Friday?
faq.answer: Freaky Friday (2003) is best described as Family, Fantasy, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Freaky Friday part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Freaky Friday Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
